content_markdown: Which graph shows the variation of amplitude with intensity for a wave?

diagram_metadata.description: Four graphs showing different relationships between amplitude (y-axis) and intensity (x-axis). Graph A shows a square root curve, Graph B shows a parabolic curve, Graph C shows a linear relationship, and Graph D shows an inverse relationship.

vibe_explanation: In physics, the intensity $I$ of a wave is proportional to the square of its amplitude $A$, expressed as $I \propto A^2$. Rearranging this for amplitude gives $A \propto \sqrt{I}$. Therefore, the graph of amplitude against intensity should follow a square root shape, which starts at the origin and increases with a decreasing gradient, as shown in graph A.
